q: ______ are the means for setting objectives on Project Planning and Tracking releases and for planning and tracking release progress towards these objectives throughout its duration.
_______are checkpoints on a release timeline used to verify that the release and its interim deliverables are progressing as planned.
A _______ contains the actions that must be performed during test execution.
A _______ is a variable that can be assigned a value during test execution.
A _______ is an independent business unit within an organization, with its own set of rules, standards, resources, objectives, and applications.

A document-centric viewing mode that enables you to see a list of requirements in a single document view and allows for quick editing of descriptions. This helps you to better understand the big picture.
A dynamic hierarchical structure based on virtual folders allows for flexibility in manipulating the Requirements view.
A good test plan enables you to assess the quality of your application at any point in the application management process.
A good test plan enables you to assess the quality of your application at any stage in the testing process.
A group of tests designed to achieve specific testing goals.
A KPI is a quantifiable measure designed to track a critical performance variable over time, and measure the essential outcome of a release scope item.
A library represents a set of entities in a project and the relationship between them.
A milestone is a point in the time-line of a release that signifies the completion of a deliverable.
A physical devices used to identify users in secure systems.
A Requirement tree is a visual ______ of the relationship between requirements.
A session/user authentication process that permits a user to enter one name and password to access multiple applications.
A subset of features for quality management teams managing medium to large release.
A test can cover more than one______ and a requirement can be covered by more than one test.
A test configuration is a set of definitions that describe a specific test use-case.
A test must be appropriate for its testers and its environment.
A test must include only the necessary steps and fields that are needed for its purpose.
A test must map to a requirement or a process.
A test set folder in the Test Lab module contains tests that are covered by your requirements.

Adds a new blank test to the Tests and Components list.
Adds a new component to the Tests and Components list.

Allows you to capture a group of requirements, tests, or test assets at strategic points in the project lifecycle to mark specific milestones.
ALM support you through ____.
a: All phases of Application Lifecycle Management

Assigns a short description of the requirement.

Displays the progress of a release in a Gantt chart.

Enable you to create and display a dynamic graphic representation of data related to test plans and test sets.

Enabled for requirements, tests, and test assets to allow distributed teams to collaborate and manage multiple versions of assets in parallel while maintaining data integrity and providing an audit history of changes throughout the project lifecycle.
Enables quality assurance managers to track application readiness by defining goals for activities of an application release.
Enables subject matter experts (SMEs) to work with Business Process Testing (BPT), the HP test automation solution.
Enables you to add and remove traceability links between requirements
Enables you to add attachments to a requirement.
Enables you to add, view, and edit rich text using an editor from within ALM
Enables you to analyze the breakdown of child requirements according to test coverage status.

Enables you to associate the selected requirement with other requirements in the requirements tree .
Enables you to control the execution of automated tests by setting test run conditions and creating a schedule.
Enables you to create and edit tests or components directly in Sprinter and save them to ALM.
Enables you to create links between requirements and other entities.
Enables you to increase the number of parallel calculations performed on an entire site.
Enables you to instruct ALM to automatically send a status alert email to the author when the test set completes execution.
Enables you to manage resources used by your tests. You can organize your resources by defining a hierarchical Test Resource tree containing resource folders and resources.
Enables you to manage the execution of manual and automated tests and document their results.
Enables you to purge calculations after a specific time period.
Enables you to record additional details about the actual test execution results.

Enables you to view and change the values of fields specified for the selected requirement

Enables you to view traceability relationships between requirements and other requirements or tests in a matrix.

Excludes the selected requirement from the conversion process

have been made in many areas of the product, such as opening, loading, and running tests.

a: The defect is indirectly linked to the related Requirement.

q: If an application flaw is detected while running a test set, you can submit a defect.

If you know that a test in your test set might fail, but you want to make sure that subsequent tests in the test set still execute, which of the following should you do?
a: Ensure that the execution condition for the arrow running out from the test is set to Finished.
